title: Tropical Vector Bundles and Matroids
speaker: Kiumars Kaveh
abstract:
A toric vector bundle is a torus equivariant vector bundle on a toric variety.
We begin by recalling the classification of toric vector bundles due to Klyachko. The Klyachko data of a toric vector bundle can be interpreted as a "piecewise linear map" from the fan of toric variety to the space of valuations on a vector space. Motivated by this, we define the notion of a "piecewise linear map" from a fan to the Bergman fan of a matroid. We call this combinatorial object a "tropical toric vector bundle" (alternatively it can be called a "toric matroid bundle"). We define equivariant K-theory and characteristic classes of these bundles. As a particular case, we see that any matroid comes with tautological tropical toric vector bundles over the permutahedral toric variety and the corresponding equivariant K-classes and Chern classes recover the tautological classes of matroids constructed in the recent work of Berget-Eur-Spink-Tseng. In analogy with toric vector bundles, one can define sheaf of sections and Euler characteristic as well as positivity notions such as global generation, ampleness and nefness for tropical toric vector bundles and prove a kind of vanishing of higher cohomologies result. This is a joint work with Christopher Manon. We note that, independently and around the same time but from a different point of view, Khan and Maclagan also have arrived at (basically) the same notion (of a tropical toric vector bundle).

proof_stages.label: Klyachko filtrations for toric vector bundles
proof_stages.summary: The speaker fixes toric notation and recalls Klyachko's classification of toric vector bundles by a distinguished fiber together with compatible integer filtrations indexed by the rays of the fan.

proof_stages.label: Filtrations as valuations and apartments
proof_stages.summary: Each filtration is recast as a non-Archimedean valuation on the fiber, and the space of all such valuations is organized as an extended Tits-building-like union of apartments indexed by bases.

proof_stages.label: Piecewise-linear maps to the building and characteristic classes
proof_stages.summary: Klyachko data are reformulated as integral piecewise-linear maps from the fan to the extended building, and equivariant Chern and K-classes are recovered by composing with symmetric functions on valuations.

proof_stages.label: Representable Bergman-factorization
proof_stages.summary: After choosing a sufficiently large spanning configuration in the fiber, the building-valued map factors through the Bergman fan of the associated representable matroid, with apartments described by circuit-minimum formulas and the data encoded in a matrix of tropical points.

proof_stages.label: Matroidal definition of tropical toric vector bundles
proof_stages.summary: The representable construction is abstracted to any matroid by defining a tropical toric vector bundle as a piecewise-linear map from a fan to the matroid Bergman fan that is linear on cones inside apartments, with an equivalent fan-free formulation using the semifield of piecewise-linear functions.

proof_stages.label: Canonical matroid bundle and tropical characteristic classes
proof_stages.summary: Every matroid yields a natural tropical toric vector bundle via the projection from the permutahedral fan to the Bergman fan, and symmetric functions on apartment coordinates define tropical equivariant Chern and K-classes agreeing with known tautological classes in the realizable case.

proof_stages.label: Sections, Euler characteristics, and vanishing
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines tropical analogues of weight spaces of sections using flats and matroid ranks, defines Euler characteristics by a Cech-style alternating sum, formulates higher-cohomology vanishing as equality with \(H^0\), and relates section counts to labeled polytopes.

proof_stages.label: Extensions and combinatorial consequences
proof_stages.summary: The final stage surveys extensions over DVRs via evaluated matroids, notions of global generation and ampleness, splitting questions, convex-chain Ehrhart theory, log-concavity of characteristic-class intersections, other tropical bases, \(\mathbb F_1\)-geometry, and principal bundles.

examples.name: Tangent bundle of projective space
examples.note: The tangent bundle of \(\mathbb P^n\), especially \(\mathbb P^2\), is used to display what Klyachko filtrations look like in a familiar toric vector bundle.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker applies Klyachko’s description to the tangent bundle of projective space/P2 by giving the corresponding filtrations and the one-dimensional subspaces for the rays
